title: STANDARD FOR QUICK-FROZEN BILBERRIES CXS 76-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2019, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR QUICK-FROZEN BILBERRIES CXS 76-1981 Adopted in 1981. Amended in 2019, 2022.
sections.summary: Document heading for the Codex Standard for Quick-Frozen Bilberries (CXS 76-1981), noting adoption in 1981 and amendments in 2019 and 2022, with a specific amendment to Section 6.4 on labelling of non-retail containers.

Scope: applies to quick-frozen bilberries (Vaccinium myrtillus L.) offered for direct consumption (except when labelled for further processing or covered by the quick-frozen blueberries standard) and permits repacking when required.

Description of quick-frozen bilberries covering product definition, required freezing process (rapid passage through crystallization and attainment of −18 °C at the thermal centre), permitted repacking, handling to maintain quality per the Quick Frozen Foods Code of Practice (CXC 8-1976), and presentation styles (free‑flowing or block).
Product definition: quick-frozen bilberries are made from fresh, clean, sound, ripe Vaccinium myrtillus L. of firm texture, with or without dry sugar or syrup, packaged and frozen appropriately.

Process definition: product must be quick-frozen so the temperature of maximum crystallization is passed quickly and the thermal centre reaches −18 °C after stabilization; repacking under controlled conditions is permitted.

Handling practice: the product must be handled during transport, storage and distribution so as to maintain quality up to final sale, and it is recommended to follow the Code of Practice for Processing and Handling of Quick Frozen Foods (CXC 8-1976).
Presentation: quick-frozen bilberries may be presented as free-flowing (individual, non-adhering berries) or non free-flowing (solid block).

Essential composition and quality factors: specifies optional sugars, compositional soluble solids limits and defect definitions, and sets detailed quality, analytical and sampling requirements (including AQL‑based lot acceptance and visual defect point tolerances) to determine acceptability.
Optional ingredients permitted are sugars (sucrose, invert sugar and syrups, dextrose, fructose, glucose syrup, dried glucose syrup).

Composition overview: sets soluble solids limits for products with dry sugar or syrup, defines a 'defective' sample unit for composition, and states lot acceptance is based on an appropriate sampling plan with AQL 6.5.
Bilberries prepared with dry sugars must have total soluble solids in the thawed, comminuted sample between 18% and 35% m/m (expressed as sucrose) as measured by refractometer at 20 °C.

Bilberries prepared with syrup: syrup only as needed to cover berries, and total soluble solids of the thawed, comminuted sample must be between 15% and 25% m/m (expressed as sucrose) as measured by refractometer at 20 °C.

Definition for composition defects: any sample unit outside the soluble solids ranges in Sections 3.2.1 or 3.2.2 is regarded as 'defective' provided its deviation beyond the specified range does not exceed 5% m/m soluble solids.

Lot acceptance for composition: a lot is acceptable if the number of composition 'defectives' (per Section 3.2.3) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5.

Quality factors: sets general visual and sensory requirements (varietal similarity, colour, freedom from foreign flavour/odour, cleanliness), analytical limits (mineral impurities ≤0.05% m/m), free‑flowing tolerance (10% m/m stuck berries), definitions of visual defects, a standard sample size of 300 g for evaluation, a point-based visual defect tolerance table, and states lot acceptance for quality factors is by an appropriate sampling plan with AQL 6.5 treating each defective individually.

Quick-frozen bilberries must be of similar varietal characteristics with good colour, free from foreign flavour/odour, clean and practically free from mould and harmless foreign matter, and, subject to specified tolerances, practically free from sand/grit, (when labelled free‑flowing) practically free from adhering berries, practically free from extraneous vegetable matter, reasonably free from cap stems, green, blemished, dissimilar, or undeveloped/mummified berries.
Mineral impurities (sand, grit and silt) in the whole product (berries and packing medium, if any) shall not exceed 0.05 percent m/m.

If presented as "free‑flowing," up to 10 percent m/m of berries stuck together and not easily separable when frozen is permitted; the sample unit for this determination is the entire contents of the container or as large a quantity as practicable.

Defines visual defect terms for bilberries: EVM (harmless leaves/plant parts), cap stems (stalks ≥2 mm), green berries (predominantly uncoloured/green cast), dissimilar varieties (other edible berries differing in colour/shape/internal characteristics), blemished (visible insect or pathological damage), and undeveloped/mummified (badly shrivelled, dried or hard berries).
The standard sample size for evaluating visual defects and applying tolerances is 300 grammes of drained berries (see Codex methods of analysis and sampling).

Visual defects are classified as "minor" or "major" and scored per Table 1 for a 300 g sample unit; a sample unit is acceptable only if it does not exceed the total allowable points (minor 20, major 10, total 20), and disintegrated/badly crushed/smashed fruit must not exceed 10% m/m.

A sample unit taken under an AQL 6.5 sampling plan is deemed "defective" if it exceeds the mineral impurity limit (Section 3.3.2), the free‑flowing tolerance (Section 3.3.3), or the total allowable points or crushed/disintegrated tolerance in Table 1 (Section 3.3.6).

A lot is acceptable for quality when the number of "defectives" (per Section 3.3.7) does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an AQL 6.5 sampling plan, with each defective treated individually for the respective characteristic.
Food additives: none permitted.

Preparation and handling should follow the General Principles of Food Hygiene and other relevant Codex codes; the product shall be free from objectionable matter as far as good manufacturing practice allows and, when tested, free from microorganisms, parasites, and microbial substances in amounts that may represent a health hazard.

In addition to the General Standard for Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods, specific labelling requirements apply for quick‑frozen bilberries covering the name, packing medium, designation as "quick‑frozen" (with a permitted "frozen" exception), storage/thawing instructions, and provisions for bulk and non‑retail containers.
Labeling specifics: the product name is "bilberries" and the label must also show the packing medium and the words "quick‑frozen" (with a limited allowance for the term "frozen").

sections.section: 6.1.1 The name of the product as declared on the label is "bilberries".
sections.summary: The name of the product as declared on the label must be "bilberries."

sections.section: 6.1.2 There shall appear on the label in conjunction with or in close proximity to the word "bilberries", the packing medium: "with (name of sweetener and whether as such or as the syrup)".
sections.summary: The label must state the packing medium in conjunction with the word "bilberries" as "with (name of sweetener and whether as such or as the syrup)."

sections.section: 6.1.3 In addition, there shall appear on the label the words "quick-frozen", except that the term "frozen" i  where this term is customarily used for describing the product processed in accordance with Section 2.2 of this standard.
sections.summary: The label must include the words "quick‑frozen," except that the term "frozen" may be used where it is customarily used for products processed per Section 2.2 of this standard.
Packages must bear clear directions for keeping the product from purchase to use and must provide directions for thawing.

For bulk quick‑frozen bilberries the required information may be on the container or in accompanying documents, but the food name with "quickfrozen" (or "frozen" per Section 6.1.3) and the manufacturer/packer name and address must appear on the container.
Labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).

Packaging for quick‑frozen bilberries must protect organoleptic and other quality characteristics, prevent microbiological and other contamination, guard against dehydration and leakage where practicable, and not impart odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics to the product.

sections.section: 7.1 Packaging used for quick-frozen bilberries shall
sections.summary: Packaging must (a) protect organoleptic and other quality characteristics, (b) protect against microbiological and other contamination, (c) protect from dehydration and leakage as far as technologically practicable, and (d) not transfer odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics to the product.

sections.section: 7.1 Packaging used for quick-frozen bilberries shall - (a) protect the organoleptic and other quality characteristics of the product;
sections.summary: Requires packaging for quick-frozen bilberries to protect the product's organoleptic and other quality characteristics.

sections.section: 7.1 Packaging used for quick-frozen bilberries shall - (b) protect the product against microbiological and other contamination;
sections.summary: Requires packaging for quick-frozen bilberries to protect the product against microbiological and other contamination.

sections.section: 7.1 Packaging used for quick-frozen bilberries shall - (c) protect  the  product  from  dehydration  and,  where  appropriate,  leakage  as  far  as  technologically practicable; and
sections.summary: Requires packaging for quick-frozen bilberries to protect the product from dehydration and, where appropriate, leakage to the extent technologically practicable.

sections.section: 7.1 Packaging used for quick-frozen bilberries shall - (d) not pass on to the product any odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics.
sections.summary: Prohibits packaging from imparting any odour, taste, colour or other foreign characteristics to quick-frozen bilberries.

Specifies that compliance with this standard must be checked using the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions, and notes that 'frozen' may be used as an alternative to 'quick-frozen' in some English-speaking countries.
